Funded by Open Philanthropy, Charlottes och Fredriks Stiftelse (Swedish family foundation), and US private donors. Also operates Axiom Futures program focused on Indian talent development. Provides comprehensive financial packages including salary, housing, and research costs.

name: Impact Research Groups
notes: 8-week mentored research program for London students run by Arcadia Impact. Operates 5 research streams: AI governance, technical AI safety, global health, biosecurity, and animal welfare. Led by Alicia Pollard. Awards £2,000 prize for best project. Part of broader Arcadia Impact ecosystem including LASR Labs and AI Safety Engineering Taskforce.

name: ML Alignment & Theory Scholars (MATS)
notes: Founded 2021 as independent research fellowship program. 146 employees across 18 countries, led by Co-Executive Directors Ryan Kidd and Christian Smith. Runs 12-week fully-funded research fellowships connecting scholars with mentors from Anthropic, OpenAI, Google DeepMind, UK AISI, and other leading AI safety organizations. Has supported 450+ researchers with 80% of pre-2025 alumni now working in AI safety. Alumni founded organizations like Apollo Research, Leap Labs, and joined top AI safety teams. Funded by Open Philanthropy, Survival and Flourishing Fund, and other philanthropic sources.

name: Non-Trivial
notes: Founded 2022 as Effective Ventures project. 8-week online fellowship for ages 14-20 tackling global challenges. Awards up to $30,000 in scholarships annually, with over $400,000 distributed since founding. Funded primarily by Open Philanthropy. Alumni include UN speakers and winners of major science competitions. 40 employees, 1,856 LinkedIn followers.

name: Pivotal Research Fellowship
notes: Founded 2021, operated by Pivotal Research organization with 16 employees. 9-week in-person fellowship program hosted at London Initiative for Safe AI (LISA). 7th cohort running 2026 Q1. Fellows receive £6-8k stipend plus housing/travel. Mentors from top AI safety organizations including Redwood Research, Google DeepMind, SecureBio, FAR AI. Program rated 8.7/10 by fellows with 88 Net Promoter Score. Research outputs include papers on AI liability, interpretability, safety evaluations.

name: PIBBSS Fellowship (Principles of Intelligent Behavior in Biological and Social Systems)
notes: Founded 2022, operates as 501(c)(3) nonprofit. Runs 3-month summer fellowship program with ~20 fellows annually, targeting PhD/postdoc researchers from diverse fields for AI safety career transitions. Funded by SFF, LTFF, Cooperative AI Fund, Foresight Institute. Key outcomes include fellows transitioning to AI safety labs, UK AISI, and academic positions. 2024 program produced multiple publication-ready research outputs in mechanistic interpretability, cooperative AI, and formal safety methods.

name: AI Alignment Awards
notes: Founded 2022 by Olivia Jimenez and Akash Wasil. Ran research contests offering up to $100,000 in prizes for solutions to AI alignment problems. Funded by Open Philanthropy, fiscally sponsored by Player's Philanthropy Fund. Final contest deadline was May 1, 2023, with judging concluding afterward. Has Discord community for participants and maintains educational resources on alignment research.

name: AI Safety Communications Centre
notes: Communications organization that connected journalists to AI safety experts and resources. Received $288,000 from Open Philanthropy in 2023 for general support. Key experts include Robert Trager, Lennart Heim, and Seán ÓhÉigeartaigh who co-authored proposal for International AI Organization. Listed as 'no longer active' on AISafety.com map as of 2024.

name: AI Safety Support Newsletter
notes: Newsletter was part of AI Safety Support (AISS), founded 2020 by Linda Linsefors and JJ Hepburn. Australian registered charity providing fiscal sponsorship, career advice, and field-building activities. Newsletter discontinued due to lack of maintainer, but parent organization continues other AI safety support activities including managing Alignment Slack and running events like Virtual AI Safety Unconference.

name: Campaign for AI Safety
notes: Founded 2023 as Australian nonprofit, now merged with Existential Risk Observatory. Conducted message testing campaigns, billboard advertisements, and policy submissions to governments worldwide including UN, US, UK, EU, and Australia. Organized international protests and collaborated with PauseAI movement. Ran international law student competition to draft AI moratorium treaty. Small team of 2 employees with focus on public advocacy and policy submissions.

name: generative.ink
notes: Personal research blog by janus, focusing on AI safety implications of large language models. Known for influential 'Simulators' post on LessWrong introducing new framework for understanding GPT-like systems. Last posted in 2023. Describes self as 'GPT cyborg' and conducts conceptual research on alignment challenges unique to generative models trained on self-supervised objectives.

name: ML & AI Safety Updates
notes: Weekly podcast, YouTube channel and newsletter that provided updates on AI safety research and developments. Listed as 'no longer active' on AISafety.com map, discontinued due to organizational reprioritization. Was featured on the Alignment Forum and served as a media source for the AI safety community to stay informed about latest research and policy developments.

name: Stop AGI
notes: Public education website focused on communicating AGI extinction risks to general audiences. Provides detailed policy proposals including global moratoriums, compute caps, and international AI safety institutions. Listed as 'no longer active' on AISafety.com map but website remains operational with recent content updates.

name: AI Safety Awareness Project (AISAP)
notes: Volunteer-run nonprofit organization with 4 employees. Operates under AI Safety Awareness Foundation (also known as AISAP). Conducts workshops nationwide at libraries, universities, and community centers on AI basics, safety evaluations, policy analysis, and forecasting. Partners with Seattle Public Library and other institutions for public education programs. Featured in AISafety.com field map as advocacy organization.

name: Advanced Research + Invention Agency (ARIA)
notes: UK government R&D agency established 2023 by Act of Parliament, modeled after DARPA. £800M budget over 5 years (£184M allocated 2025-26). CEO Ilan Gur, 95 employees. Eight program directors with £50M+ budgets each. Major programs: Safeguarded AI (£69M), Scaling Compute (£50M), Precision Neurotechnologies. Portfolio includes AI hardware startups like Fractile (£5M) and Callosum (partnership). Operates independently from government intervention.

name: Institute for Law & AI (LawAI)
notes: Founded as independent think tank, formerly Legal Priorities Project. Staff of 40+ researchers across 12 countries, led by Founding Director Christoph Winter. Publishes working paper series via SSRN, produces policy reports and research articles on AI governance. Notable recent work includes 'Legal Alignment for Safe and Ethical AI' and 'Automated Compliance and the Regulation of AI'. Hosts workshops on law-following AI and maintains research collaborations with academic institutions.

name: International AI Governance Alliance (IAIGA)
notes: Founded 2025 as initiative of Center for Existential Safety. Being incorporated as nonprofit in Geneva. Launched petition calling for supranational AI governance organization, aiming for 1M signatures. Led by James Norris who has 26+ years preparing for Singularity. Expects AI 'warning shot' or catastrophe soon. Seeks to develop vetted treaty quickly as 'Plan B' to other multilateral efforts.

name: International Association for Safe & Ethical AI (IASEAI)
notes: Founded in 2017 as international nonprofit coalition, significantly expanded in 2024 with 23 employees (+350% YoY growth). Received $1.85M grant from Future of Life Institute in 2025. Hosts major annual conferences (IASEAI'25 had 700+ attendees from 65 countries, IASEAI'26 had 1300+ from 99+ countries). Advises UN, OECD, EU on AI governance and helped shape EU AI Act. Develops practical tools including AI scorecards, ethical certification programs, and Safe AI Deployment Toolkit (SAIDT). Key leaders include Stuart Russell (President) and Mark Nitzberg (Interim Executive Director).

name: Centre pour la Sécurité de l'IA (CeSIA)
notes: Founded March 2024 by Charbel-Raphaël Ségerie and team. ~20 employees plus volunteers. Funded by Survival and Flourishing Fund, Longview Philanthropy, Swedish foundation, Effektiv Spenden. No AI company funding to avoid conflicts. Provides Europe's first university AI safety courses at ENS Ulm/Paris-Saclay. Initiated Global Call for AI Red Lines with 300+ signatories including Nobel laureates. Co-organized symposiums with Yoshua Bengio and Stuart Russell.

name: 5050 AI (by Fifty Years)
notes: Free 13-week startup accelerator program run by Fifty Years VC, helping scientists and engineers build AI safety/alignment companies. 445 participants to date, launched 105 companies with 96% fundraising success rate. Mentors include OpenAI co-founder Wojciech Zaremba, former OpenAI interim CEO Emmett Shear. UK program launched in partnership with ARIA in 2025. No equity taken, entirely free program focused on commercializing AI safety research.

name: Centre for Enabling EA Learning & Research (CEEALAR)
notes: Founded 2018 by Greg Colbourn, registered charity in England & Wales since 2020. Provides subsidized accommodation and workspace in Blackpool for EA researchers and entrepreneurs at £750/month. Supports ~20 residents at a time with 4-6 month average stays. 66 grantees in 2025 (+36% YoY). Notable alumni include Chris Leong (Principal Organiser for AI Safety New Zealand) and Hoagy Cunningham (Stanford Existential Risks Initiative). Formerly known as 'EA Hotel', rebranded in 2020. Currently facing £330k funding gap and needs £30k for urgent roof repairs.

name: AI Governance and Safety Institute (AIGSI)
notes: The AI Governance and Safety Institute (AIGSI) is a nonpartisan 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization on a mission to ensure artificial intelligence and other technologies benefit humanity and are developed safely, securely, and in alignment with human values. The organization aims to improve institutional response to the risk that future AI might cause human extinction and ensure the enormous potential benefits of AI are realized. They conduct research, engage in outreach, and educate stakeholders and the general public. The organization focuses on key technical concepts including machine learning interpretability, outer alignment, inner alignment, convergent instrumental subgoals, and advanced agents.

name: AI Safety Asia (AISA)
notes: Founded 2024 by Supheakmungkol Sarin (former Google AI lead and World Economic Forum Head of Data & AI) and Edward Tsoi. Team of 13 employees across 7 countries. Has engaged 2,000+ AI governance professionals across 16 Asian countries. Co-hosted sessions at India AI Impact Summit 2026 on crisis diplomacy and contributed to International AI Safety Report 2026. Focuses on building durable governance infrastructure through regional research, structured peer learning, and implementation-oriented engagement.

name: Athena Mentorship Program for Women (AI Alignment Research)
notes: Founded 2023 by Claire Short. 10-week remote mentorship program with 1-week in-person retreat. Targets women transitioning into or early in AI alignment research careers. Funded through Manifund ($20k goal reached), Lightspeed grants. Requires 20+ hours/week commitment. Provides mentorship from established AI safety researchers, weekly research talks, networking opportunities, and potential placement in research groups post-program. Has run multiple cohorts with positive participant feedback.

name: Institute for AI Policy and Strategy (IAPS) AI Policy Fellowship
notes: Nonpartisan think tank producing policy research on AI implications. Fellowship program runs 3 months with $15,000-$22,000 stipends, training professionals from varied backgrounds in AI policy. Alumni have secured positions at RAND, Institute for Progress, Center for Health Security, and government agencies. Recent research includes strategic visions in AI governance and policy recommendations for the AI Action Plan. Features mentorship from leading experts and produces concrete policy outputs like briefings to government officials and international conferences.

name: Orion AI Governance Initiative (Arcadia Impact)
notes: Founded 2024 as part of Arcadia Impact. UK-based talent development scheme with 3 employees. Offers 9-week AI Policy Accelerator program, mentorship scheme, and internship placements. Has trained 57 participants across programs and placed 2 people into AI policy roles at think tanks. Applications for 2026 program closed January 6, 2026. Project manager is Callum Hinchcliffe, who previously worked on AI governance research and policy.

name: Pathfinder Fellowship (Kairos)
notes: Launched 2025 by Kairos, replacing Open Philanthropy's University Organizer Fellowship and Kairos's prior Fieldbuilder Support Program. Supports ~70 AI safety university groups worldwide with funding, mentorship, and resources. 12 staff across 7 countries. Funded by Open Philanthropy grant, fiscally sponsored through BERI. Provides $500+ startup funding plus ongoing support and graduate student stipends.

name: Technical Alignment Research Accelerator (TARA)
notes: Founded 2024 by AI Safety Australia & New Zealand (AIS ANZ), led by Yanni Kyriacos. 14-week part-time program based on ARENA curriculum with weekly in-person Saturday sessions. Inaugural cohort targeted 25 participants across Australian cities. Funded by $14,901 grant from Ryan Kidd via Manifund. Expanding to Singapore, Manila, Tokyo, and Taipei in 2026. Program aims to develop technical talent for AI Safety Institutes and demonstrate community credibility to Australian government.

name: Center for AI Policy (CAIP)
notes: Founded 2023, shut down in September 2025 due to lack of funding. Was a 501(c)(4) advocacy organization that conducted over 406 meetings with Congressional staffers, hosted 8 congressional briefings reaching 150+ staffers, and developed the 'Responsible AI Act' model legislation. Funded primarily by Survival and Flourishing Fund. Executive Director Jason Green-Lowe continues to offer legislative review services and seeks funding to revive the organization.
From AISafety.com map. 12/14-week program run by Fifty Years, helping scientists and engineers build startups working on AI safety.

name: Vector Institute
notes: Toronto-based nonprofit AI research institute founded in 2017, focused on machine learning and deep learning. Funded jointly by the Government of Ontario, the federal Pan-Canadian AI Strategy (CIFAR), and industry sponsors. New President & CEO Glenda Crisp appointed April 2025.

name: Stanford HAI
notes: Stanford HAI (Stanford Institute for Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence) is an interdisciplinary research institute at Stanford University, founded in 2019 by Fei-Fei Li and John Etchemendy, along with James Landay and Chris Manning. The institute advances AI research, education, policy, and practice with the goal of ensuring AI benefits humanity. HAI publishes the annual AI Index Report — the most widely cited benchmark of global AI progress, tracking technical capability, industry investment, policy activity, and public opinion. It has invested over $40 million across 300+ Stanford researchers spanning computer science, social sciences, law, and medicine. In the U.S. AI policy ecosystem, HAI occupies an influential centrist position: its co-directors, fellows, and researchers regularly advise Congress, federal agencies, and international governance bodies. The institute advocates for AI that augments rather than replaces humans, and for targeted federal oversight rather than either blanket restriction or unchecked acceleration.

name: Time (AI coverage)
notes: Time magazine has extensively covered AI developments, with correspondents Andrew R. Chow and Billy Perrigo leading AI coverage through their newsletter 'In the Loop'. The publication has launched TIME AI initiatives including the TIME AI Agent, developed in collaboration with Scale AI, which provides personalized, interactive journalism experiences. Time has also partnered with OpenAI to advance global AI literacy and created AI-powered features like daily audio briefs and translation services. The magazine named 'The Architects of AI' as the 2025 Person of the Year, reflecting AI's significant impact.

name: Leverhulme Centre for the Future of Intelligence
notes: From AISafety.com map. Interdisciplinary research center at the University of Cambridge exploring the nature, ethics, and impact of AI.

name: Collective Action for Existential Safety
notes: From AISafety.com map. Aiming to catalyze collective effort towards reducing existential risk, including through an extensive action list for individuals, organizations, and nations.

name: GovAI Fellowship
notes: From AISafety.com map. 3-month program by the Centre for the Governance of AI, designed to help professionals transition to working on AI governance.

name: Heron
notes: From AISafety.com map. Working to bridge the gap between frontier AI models and the level of cybersecurity they need by connecting professionals to high-leverage opportunities in AI security.

name: Computational Rational Agents Laboratory
notes: From AISafety.com map. Research group studying agent foundations in order to create the mathematical tools to align the objectives of an AI with human values.

name: Dovetail
notes: From AISafety.com map. Research group working on foundational mathematics that provides an understanding of the nature of AI agents.

name: Association for Long Term Existence and Resilience
notes: From AISafety.com map. Israeli research and advocacy nonprofit working to investigate, demonstrate, and foster useful ways to safeguard and improve the future of humanity.

name: Computational and Biological Learning Lab
notes: From AISafety.com map. Research group using engineering approaches to understand the brain and to develop artificial learning systems.

name: Krueger AI Safety Lab
notes: From AISafety.com map. AI safety research group at Mila, led by David Krueger. Previously based at the University of Cambridge.

name: Model Evaluation & Threat Research
notes: From AISafety.com map. Researching, developing, and running evaluations of AI capabilities, including broad autonomous capabilities and the ability of AI systems to conduct AI R&D.

name: NYU Alignment Research Group
notes: From AISafety.com map. Group of researchers at New York University doing empirical work with LLMs in order to address risks posed by advanced AI.

name: Quantified Uncertainty Research Institute
notes: From AISafety.com map. Advancing forecasting and epistemics to improve the long-term future of humanity. Conducts research and builds software.

name: AI Safety Tactical Opportunities Fund
notes: From AISafety.com map. Pooled multi-donor fund structured to be fast and rapidly capture emerging opportunities, including in governance, technical alignment, and evaluations. Managed by JueYan Zhang.

name: AI2050
notes: From AISafety.com map. Philanthropic initiative supporting researchers working on key opportunities and hard problems that are critical to get right for society to benefit from AI.

name: Science of Trustworthy AI
notes: From AISafety.com map. Funder housing the science-focused philanthropic efforts of Eric and Wendy Schmidt, moving large amounts of funding towards AI safety via its AI institute.

name: Centre pour la Sécurité de l'IA
notes: From AISafety.com map. French nonprofit dedicated to research, education, and awareness-raising about the most extreme risks of advanced AI.

name: Berkeley Existential Risk Initiative
notes: From AISafety.com map. Providing flexible funding and operations support to university research groups working on existential risk, enabling projects otherwise hindered by university administration.
